title: Treat failure detection as a suspicion, not a fact
description: Build failure detectors that report suspicion with timeouts and backoff, accepting that you cannot distinguish a slow node from a dead one.
body: Use when nodes must react to peers going down (election triggers, membership changes). Do not treat a single missed heartbeat as proof of death, and do not pick timeouts so tight that a GC pause looks like a crash. Actions: use heartbeats with adaptive timeouts (e.g. phi-accrual), require multiple missed beats before suspecting, and make the protocol safe even when a 'dead' node is actually alive and returns. Validation signals include a slow-node test where the cluster stays correct without falsely evicting it and tunable suspicion thresholds. Risk boundary: aggressive eviction of a slow-but-live primary can trigger split-brain or data loss; escalate before tightening detection timeouts on a stateful service.
